import urllib.error
from urllib.parse import urlparse
from typing import Dict, Any
import re
import ssl
import certifi


def fetch_page_metadata(page_url: str) -> dict:
    """
    Fetches the title and description metadata from the specified web page URL.

    Args:
        page_url: A valid URL pointing to a public web page. Example: 'https://example.com/article'

    Returns:
        A dictionary containing the page's title and description, e.g., {'title': 'Example', 'description': 'An example page'}
    """
    # Validate URL format and protocol
    parsed_url = urlparse(page_url)
    if not all([parsed_url.scheme in ('http', 'https'), parsed_url.netloc]):
        return {
            'title': 'Invalid URL',
            'description': 'URL must be a valid HTTP or HTTPS address'
        }
    
    # Check for private IP ranges to prevent SSRF
    netloc = parsed_url.netloc.split(':')[0]  # Remove port if present
    if _is_private_ip(netloc):
        return {
            'title': 'Access denied',
            'description': 'Cannot access private/local addresses'
        }

    try:
        # Create a request object with security headers
        req = urllib.request.Request(
            page_url,
            headers={
                'User-Agent': 'Mozilla/5.0 (compatible; SocialMediaScraper/1.0)'
            }
        )
        
        # Fetch the content of the specified URL
        context = ssl.create_default_context(cafile=certifi.where())
        with urllib.request.urlopen(req, context=context, timeout=10) as response:
            content = response.read().decode('utf-8')

        # Extract title - using more secure regex pattern
        title_match = re.search(r'<title>\s*(.*?)\s*</title>', content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL)
        title = _clean_text(title_match.group(1)) if title_match else "No title found"

        # Extract meta description - using more secure regex pattern
        desc_match = re.search(
            r'<meta\s+(?:[^>]*?\s+)?name\s*=\s*["\']description["\'](?:\s+[^>]*)?\s+content\s*=\s*["\']([^"\']*)["\']',
            content, 
            re.IGNORECASE
        )
        if not desc_match:
            # Also check for property attribute (like og:description)
            desc_match = re.search(
                r'<meta\s+(?:[^>]*?\s+)?property\s*=\s*["\']og:description["\'](?:\s+[^>]*)?\s+content\s*=\s*["\']([^"\']*)["\']',
                content, 
                re.IGNORECASE
            )
        
        description = _clean_text(desc_match.group(1)) if desc_match else "No description available"

        return {
            'title': title,
            'description': description
        }

    except (urllib.error.URLError, urllib.error.HTTPError, UnicodeDecodeError, ValueError):
        # If the page cannot be fetched or parsed, return default values
        return {
            'title': 'Content unavailable',
            'description': 'Unable to fetch page content'
        }


def _is_private_ip(hostname: str) -> bool:
    """Check if hostname resolves to a private IP address."""
    import socket
    try:
        # First check if hostname itself looks like a private address
        if any([
            hostname.startswith(('10.', '172.', '192.')),
            hostname == 'localhost' or hostname.startswith('127.'),
            hostname.endswith(('.internal', '.local', '.lan')),
            hostname == '0.0.0.0'
        ]):
            return True
        
        # Resolve hostname to IP
        ip = socket.gethostbyname(hostname)
        
        # Check for private IP ranges
        parts = ip.split('.')
        if len(parts) == 4:
            first_octet = int(parts[0])
            second_octet = int(parts[1]) if len(parts) > 1 else 0
            
            if (
                first_octet == 10 or
                (first_octet == 172 and 16 <= second_octet <= 31) or
                (first_octet == 192 and second_octet == 168) or
                first_octet == 127 or
                first_octet == 0
            ):
                return True
    except (socket.gaierror, ValueError):
        # If hostname cannot be resolved, assume it's not private
        pass
    
    return False


def _clean_text(text: str) -> str:
    """Clean extracted text by removing extra whitespace and HTML entities."""
    import html
    # Decode HTML entities
    text = html.unescape(text)
    # Remove extra whitespace while preserving single spaces
    text = ' '.join(text.split())
    return text

        return ip.is_private or ip.is_loopback or ip.is_link_local

            'local',

            return True
            
        # Check for common local/private domains
        if hostname.lower().endswith(('.local', '.internal', '.corp', '.lan')):
            return True
            
        return False
    return False

import urllib.error
from urllib.parse import urlparse
from typing import Dict, Any

def fetch_page_metadata(page_url: str) -> dict:
    """
    Fetches the title and description metadata from the specified web page URL.

    Args:
        page_url: A valid URL pointing to a public web page. Example: 'https://example.com/article'

    Returns:
        A dictionary containing the page's title and description, e.g., {'title': 'Example', 'description': 'An example page'}
    """
    try:
        # Validate the URL to prevent SSRF attacks
        parsed_url = validate_url(page_url)
        
        # Create a request with security restrictions
        context = ssl.create_default_context(cafile=certifi.where())
        
        # Disable redirects by creating a custom HTTP handler
        class NoRedirectHandler(urllib.request.HTTPRedirectHandler):
            def redirect_request(self, req, fp, code, msg, headers, newurl):
                raise urllib.error.HTTPError(req.full_url, code, "Redirects not allowed", headers, fp)
        
        no_redirect_opener = urllib.request.build_opener(NoRedirectHandler)
        
        # Create request object to set additional restrictions
        req = urllib.request.Request(
            page_url,
            headers={'User-Agent': 'Mozilla/5.0 (compatible; SocialMediaScraper/1.0)'}
        )
        
        # For HTTPS URLs, use the SSL context; for HTTP URLs, don't pass the context
        if parsed_url.scheme == 'https':
            with no_redirect_opener.open(req, timeout=10, context=context) as response:
                content = response.read().decode('utf-8')
        else:
            with no_redirect_opener.open(req, timeout=10) as response:
                content = response.read().decode('utf-8')

        # Extract title
        title_match = re.search(r'<title>(.*?)</title>', content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL)
        title = title_match.group(1).strip() if title_match else "No title found"

        # Extract meta description
        desc_match = re.search(r'<meta[^>]*name=["\']description["\'][^>]*content=["\'](.*?)["\']',
                              content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL)
        description = desc_match.group(1).strip() if desc_match else "No description available"

        return {
            'title': title,
            'description': description
        }

    except (urllib.error.URLError, urllib.error.HTTPError, UnicodeDecodeError, ValueError):
        # Return default values if the page cannot be fetched or parsed
        return {
            'title': 'Content unavailable',
            'description': 'Unable to fetch page content'
        }


def validate_url(url: str) -> bool:
    """Validates a URL to prevent SSRF attacks."""
    parsed = urlparse(url)
    
    # Only allow http and https schemes
    if parsed.scheme not in ['http', 'https']:
        raise ValueError("Invalid URL scheme")
    
    # Check hostname - resolve to IP and validate it's not private/local
    hostname = parsed.hostname
    
    if not hostname:
        raise ValueError("Invalid hostname")
    
    # Handle various forms of IP addresses and special cases
    try:
        # Check if it's an IPv4 address
        ip = ip_address(hostname)
        if ip.is_private or ip.is_loopback or ip.is_link_local:
            raise ValueError("URL points to private/local network")
    except ValueError:
        # It's not an IPv4 address, might be IPv6 or domain name
        try:
            # Check for IPv6 addresses
            ip = ip_address(socket.gethostbyname(hostname))
            if ip.is_private or ip.is_loopback or ip.is_link_local:
                raise ValueError("URL points to private/local network")
        except socket.gaierror:
            # If DNS resolution fails, we still want to check for common local patterns
            normalized_hostname = hostname.lower().strip()
            
            # Check for numeric IPs represented in different formats
            # Convert to IP address if possible and check
            try:
                # Check for decimal IP representation like 2130706433 (127.0.0.1 as integer)
                if hostname.isdigit():
                    num_ip = int(hostname)
                    if 2130706433 <= num_ip <= 2147549183:  # 127.0.0.1 to 127.255.255.255
                        raise ValueError("URL points to private/local network")
                
                # Check for octal IP representation
                elif re.match(r'^0\d+', hostname):
                    # Octal representation
                    if hostname.startswith('0177'):  # 0177.x.x.x corresponds to 127.x.x.x
                        raise ValueError("URL points to private/local network")
                
                # Check for hex IP representation
                elif hostname.startswith('0x'):
                    try:
                        hex_ip = int(hostname, 16)
                        if 2130706433 <= hex_ip <= 2147549183:  # 127.0.0.1 to 127.255.255.255
                            raise ValueError("URL points to private/local network")
                    except ValueError:
                        pass
                
                # Check for common local hostnames after normalizing
                if normalized_hostname in ['localhost', 'localhost.localdomain', 'broadcasthost'] or \
                   normalized_hostname.startswith('127.') or \
                   normalized_hostname.startswith('10.') or \
                   normalized_hostname.startswith('192.168.') or \
                   normalized_hostname.startswith('172.'):
                    # Resolve the hostname to check actual IPs
                    try:
                        resolved_ips = socket.getaddrinfo(hostname, None, socket.AF_INET)
                        for res in resolved_ips:
                            ip = ip_address(res[4][0])
                            if ip.is_private or ip.is_loopback or ip.is_link_local:
                                raise ValueError("URL resolves to private/local network")
                    except socket.gaierror:
                        raise ValueError("Could not resolve hostname")
                        
            except ValueError as ve:
                if "private/local network" in str(ve):
                    raise ve
                # Otherwise continue with other checks
            
    # Additional check for IPv6 loopback addresses
    if hostname == '[::1]' or '[::ffff:127.' in hostname or hostname in ['[::]', '[0:0:0:0:0:0:0:1]']:
        raise ValueError("URL points to private/local network")
    
    # Block common internal services patterns
    if any(blocked in url.lower() for blocked in [
        'docker.sock', 'metadata.google.internal', '169.254.169.254'
    ]):
        raise ValueError("URL contains blocked patterns")
    
    return parsed

import ssl
import certifi


def fetch_page_metadata(page_url: str) -> dict:
    """
    Fetches the title and description metadata from the specified web page URL.

    Args:
        page_url: A valid URL pointing to a public web page. Example: 'https://example.com/article'

    Returns:
        A dictionary containing the page's title and description, e.g., {'title': 'Example', 'description': 'An example page'}
    """

        }

                'Accept': 'text/html,application/xhtml+xml,application/xml;q=0.9,*/*;q=0.8'

        context = ssl.create_default_context(cafile=certifi.where())

            if len(content) > 10 * 1024 * 1024:  # 10MB limit
                return {
                    'title': 'Content too large',

                }
                
            content = content.decode('utf-8', errors='ignore')

        # Extract title - using a more secure approach
        title_match = re.search(r'<title>(.*?)</title>', content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL)
        title = ""
        if title_match:
            # Sanitize extracted title
            title = _sanitize_text(title_match.group(1).strip())
        else:

        # Extract meta description - using a more secure approach
        desc_match = re.search(
            r'<meta[^>]*name=["\']description["\'][^>]*content=["\'](.*?)["\'][^>]*>',
            content, 
            re.IGNORECASE
        )
        description = ""

        }

    except (urllib.error.URLError, urllib.error.HTTPError, UnicodeDecodeError, ValueError):

        return {

def _is_valid_url(url: str) -> bool:
    """
    Validates if the given URL is safe to request.
    Prevents SSRF by checking protocol, IP addresses, etc.
    """
    try:
        parsed = urlparse(url)
        
        # Only allow http and https
        if parsed.scheme not in ('http', 'https'):
            return False
            
        # Disallow private/local IP addresses to prevent SSRF
        hostname = parsed.hostname
        if not hostname:
            return False
            
        # Check for private IP ranges and localhost
        if _is_private_ip(hostname):
            return False
            
        # Additional check for encoded IPs (like 127.0.0.1 as decimal)
        import socket
        try:
            ip = socket.gethostbyname(hostname)
            if _is_private_ip(ip):
                return False
        except socket.gaierror:
            pass  # Could not resolve hostname, let's continue validation
            
        return True
    except Exception:
        return False


def _is_private_ip(hostname: str) -> bool:
    """
    Checks if the hostname resolves to a private IP address.
    """
    import socket
    import ipaddress
    
    try:
        # Handle direct IP addresses
        try:
            ip_obj = ipaddress.ip_address(hostname)
            return ip_obj.is_private or ip_obj.is_loopback or ip_obj.is_link_local
        except ValueError:
            # Not a direct IP, try resolving hostname
            ip = socket.gethostbyname(hostname)
            ip_obj = ipaddress.ip_address(ip)
            return ip_obj.is_private or ip_obj.is_loopback or ip_obj.is_link_local
    except Exception:
        return False


def _sanitize_text(text: str) -> str:
    """
    Sanitizes extracted text to remove potentially harmful content.
    """
    # Remove any potential script tags or other dangerous elements
    # Though these should not appear in title/description normally
    sanitized = re.sub(r'<script.*?>.*?</script>', '', text, flags=re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL)
    sanitized = re.sub(r'javascript:', '', sanitized, flags=re.IGNORECASE)
    sanitized = re.sub(r'data:', '', sanitized, flags=re.IGNORECASE)
    sanitized = re.sub(r'vbscript:', '', sanitized, flags=re.IGNORECASE)
    
    # Strip whitespace and limit length
    sanitized = sanitized.strip()[:500]  # Limit to 500 chars to prevent long payloads
    
    return sanitized

def is_safe_url(url: str) -> bool:
    """
    Check if the URL is safe to access (prevent SSRF).
    - Only allow http/https protocols
    - Prevent access to private/local IPs
    """
    try:
        parsed = urllib.parse.urlparse(url)
        
        # Only allow http/https
        if parsed.scheme not in ('http', 'https'):
            return False
            
        # Get hostname
        hostname = parsed.hostname
        if not hostname:
            return False
            
        # Resolve hostname to IP to check if it's a private address
        try:
            ip_addr = socket.gethostbyname(hostname)
            # Check if the resolved IP is private
            if ipaddress.ip_address(ip_addr).is_private:
                return False
        except socket.gaierror:
            # If hostname cannot be resolved, return False
            return False
            
        # Additional checks for localhost variations
        if hostname.lower() in ('localhost', '127.0.0.1', '::1') or hostname.startswith('internal.'):
            return False
            
        return True
    except Exception:
        return False


def fetch_page_metadata(page_url: str) -> dict:
    """
    Fetch and extract metadata from a given webpage.
    
    Args:
        page_url: The URL of the webpage to scrape
        
    Returns:
        A dictionary containing 'title' and 'description' of the page
    """
    # Validate URL to prevent SSRF attacks
    if not is_safe_url(page_url):
        raise ValueError("Invalid or unsafe URL provided")
    
    # Create SSL context with certifi certificates
    context = ssl.create_default_context(cafile=certifi.where())
    
    try:
        # Make request with timeout to prevent hanging
        response = urllib.request.urlopen(
            page_url, 
            context=context, 
            timeout=10  # 10 second timeout
        )
        
        # Read and decode content
        content = response.read()
        encoding = response.headers.get_content_charset() or 'utf-8'
        
        try:
            html_content = content.decode(encoding)
        except UnicodeDecodeError:
            # Try different encodings if utf-8 fails
            html_content = content.decode('latin1')
        
        # Parse HTML content to extract metadata
        parser = MetaDataHTMLParser()
        parser.feed(html_content)
        
        return {
            'title': parser.title,
            'description': parser.description
        }
        
    except urllib.error.URLError as e:
        raise RuntimeError(f"Failed to fetch URL: {str(e)}")
    except Exception as e:
        raise RuntimeError(f"An error occurred while fetching metadata: {str(e)}")

def _is_private_address(hostname: str) -> bool:
    """Check if hostname resolves to a private IP address"""
    if not hostname:
        return False
    
    # Check for common local/private hostnames
    if hostname in ['localhost', 'localhost.localdomain']:
        return True
    
    # Check for private IP ranges
    import socket
    try:
        ip = socket.gethostbyname(hostname)
        parts = ip.split('.')
        if len(parts) == 4:
            first_octet = int(parts[0])
            second_octet = int(parts[1]) if len(parts) > 1 else 0
            
            # Private IP ranges: 10.x.x.x, 172.16.x.x-172.31.x.x, 192.168.x.x, 127.x.x.x
            if first_octet == 10 or \
               (first_octet == 172 and 16 <= second_octet <= 31) or \
               (first_octet == 192 and second_octet == 168) or \
               first_octet == 127:
                return True
    except (socket.gaierror, ValueError):
        pass
    
    return False

import urllib.request
import urllib.error
from urllib.parse import urlparse
from typing import Dict, Any
import re
import ssl
import certifi
import ipaddress
import socket


def is_safe_url(url: str) -> bool:
    """Check if the URL is safe to access by validating its scheme, domain, and IP."""
    parsed = urlparse(url)
    
    # Only allow http and https schemes
    if parsed.scheme not in ('http', 'https'):
        return False
    
    # Check for private/local addresses
    hostname = parsed.hostname
    if not hostname:
        return False
    
    # Block IP addresses (both IPv4 and IPv6)
    try:
        ipaddress.ip_address(hostname)
        return False
    except ValueError:
        pass  # Not an IP address, continue checking
    
    # Resolve hostname to check if it resolves to private IPs
    try:
        resolved_ips = socket.getaddrinfo(hostname, None)
        for res in resolved_ips:
            ip = ipaddress.ip_address(res[4][0])
            if ip.is_private or ip.is_loopback or ip.is_link_local:
                return False
    except socket.gaierror:
        return False  # Cannot resolve hostname
    
    # Block common local hostnames
    if hostname in ('localhost', 'local', 'broadcasthost') or hostname.startswith('127.'):
        return False
        
    return True


def fetch_page_metadata(page_url: str) -> dict:
    """
    Fetches the title and description metadata from the specified web page URL.

    Args:
        page_url: A valid URL pointing to a public web page. Example: 'https://example.com/article'

    Returns:
        A dictionary containing the page's title and description, e.g., {'title': 'Example', 'description': 'An example page'}
    """
    # Validate URL safety first
    if not is_safe_url(page_url):
        return {
            'title': 'Content unavailable',
            'description': 'Invalid or unsafe URL provided'
        }
    
    try:
        # Create request with security headers
        request = urllib.request.Request(
            page_url,
            headers={
                'User-Agent': 'Mozilla/5.0 (compatible; SocialMediaScraper/1.0)'
            }
        )
        
        # Set up SSL context with proper certificates
        context = ssl.create_default_context(cafile=certifi.where())
        
        # Fetch the content
        with urllib.request.urlopen(request, context=context, timeout=10) as response:
            content = response.read().decode('utf-8')

        # Extract title - more robust regex pattern
        title_match = re.search(r'<title[^>]*>(.*?)</title>', content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL)
        title = title_match.group(1).strip() if title_match else "Content unavailable"
        # Remove extra whitespace and unescape HTML entities
        title = re.sub(r'\s+', ' ', title)

        # Extract meta description - handle both single and double quotes
        desc_patterns = [
            r'<meta[^>]*name\s*=\s*["\']description["\'][^>]*content\s*=\s*["\']([^"\']*)["\'][^>]*>',
            r'<meta[^>]*content\s*=\s*["\']([^"\']*)["\'][^>]*name\s*=\s*["\']description["\'][^>]*>',
            r'<meta[^>]*property\s*=\s*["\']og:description["\'][^>]*content\s*=\s*["\']([^"\']*)["\'][^>]*>',
            r'<meta[^>]*content\s*=\s*["\']([^"\']*)["\'][^>]*property\s*=\s*["\']og:description["\'][^>]*>'
        ]
        
        description = "No description available"
        for pattern in desc_patterns:
            desc_match = re.search(pattern, content, re.IGNORECASE)
            if desc_match:
                description = desc_match.group(1).strip()
                break
        
        # Clean up description
        description = re.sub(r'\s+', ' ', description)

        return {
            'title': title,
            'description': description
        }

    except (urllib.error.URLError, urllib.error.HTTPError, UnicodeDecodeError, socket.timeout):
        # If the page cannot be fetched or parsed, return default values
        return {
            'title': 'Content unavailable',
            'description': 'Unable to fetch page content'
        }
    except Exception:

        return {
            'title': 'Content unavailable',

        }

import urllib.error
from urllib.parse import urlparse
from typing import Dict, Any
import re
import ssl
import certifi

def fetch_page_metadata(page_url: str) -> dict:
    """
    Fetches the title and description metadata from the specified web page URL.

    Args:
        page_url: A valid URL pointing to a public web page. Example: 'https://example.com/article'

    Returns:
        A dictionary containing the page's title and description, e.g., {'title': 'Example', 'description': 'An example page'}
    """
    # Validate URL format and scheme
    parsed_url = urlparse(page_url)
    if not parsed_url.scheme or parsed_url.scheme not in ('http', 'https'):
        return {
            'title': 'Invalid URL',
            'description': 'Only HTTP and HTTPS URLs are allowed'
        }
    
    # Prevent SSRF by checking host
    if not _is_valid_host(parsed_url.hostname):
        return {
            'title': 'Invalid Host',
            'description': 'Access to this host is not allowed'
        }
    
    try:
        # Create SSL context with certificate verification
        context = ssl.create_default_context(cafile=certifi.where())
        
        # Set a reasonable timeout to prevent hanging requests
        request = urllib.request.Request(
            page_url,
            headers={
                'User-Agent': 'Mozilla/5.0 (compatible; SocialMediaScraper/1.0)'
            }
        )
        
        with urllib.request.urlopen(request, context=context, timeout=10) as response:
            content = response.read().decode('utf-8', errors='ignore')

        # Extract title safely using a more secure regex pattern
        title_match = re.search(r'<title[^>]*>([^<]{0,500})</title>', content, re.IGNORECASE)
        title = title_match.group(1).strip() if title_match else "No title found"
        
        # Limit the length to prevent extremely long titles
        title = title[:200]

        # Extract meta description with improved regex
        desc_match = re.search(
            r'<meta[^>]*(?:name|property)=["\'](?:description|og:description)["\'][^>]*content=["\']([^"\']{0,1000})["\']',
            content, 
            re.IGNORECASE
        )
        description = desc_match.group(1).strip() if desc_match else "No description available"
        
        # Limit description length
        description = description[:500]

        return {
            'title': title,
            'description': description
        }

    except (urllib.error.URLError, urllib.error.HTTPError, UnicodeDecodeError, ValueError, socket.timeout):
        # If the page cannot be fetched or parsed, return default values
        return {
            'title': 'Content unavailable',
            'description': 'Unable to fetch page content'
        }


def _is_valid_host(hostname: str) -> bool:
    """
    Validates that the hostname is not an internal/private address.
    """
    if not hostname:
        return False
    
    # Block IP addresses (IPv4 and IPv6)
    try:
        # Try to parse as IPv4
        socket.inet_aton(hostname)
        return False  # If it's a valid IP, block it
    except socket.error:
        pass
    
    try:
        # Try to parse as IPv6
        socket.inet_pton(socket.AF_INET6, hostname)
        return False  # If it's a valid IPv6, block it
    except socket.error:
        pass
    
    # Block common internal hostnames
    if hostname in ['localhost', 'localhost.localdomain']:
        return False
    
    # Check if hostname resolves to private IP
    try:
        ip_addresses = socket.getaddrinfo(hostname, None)
        for addr_info in ip_addresses:
            ip = addr_info[4][0]
            if _is_private_ip(ip):
                return False
    except socket.gaierror:
        # If DNS resolution fails, we still proceed (though it would fail later anyway)
        pass
    
    return True


def _is_private_ip(ip_str: str) -> bool:
    """
    Checks if the given IP address is in private ranges.
    """
    try:
        ip = socket.inet_aton(ip_str)
        # Convert to unsigned integer
        ip_int = int.from_bytes(ip, byteorder='big')
        
        # Private IP ranges
        # 10.0.0.0/8
        if (ip_int >> 24) == 10:
            return True
        # 172.16.0.0/12
        if ((ip_int >> 24) & 0xFF) == 172 and (((ip_int >> 16) & 0xFF) >= 16 and ((ip_int >> 16) & 0xFF) <= 31):
            return True
        # 192.168.0.0/16
        if ((ip_int >> 24) & 0xFF) == 192 and ((ip_int >> 16) & 0xFF) == 168:
            return True
        # 127.0.0.0/8 (loopback)
        if ((ip_int >> 24) & 0xFF) == 127:
            return True
        # 169.254.0.0/16 (link-local)
        if ((ip_int >> 24) & 0xFF) == 169 and ((ip_int >> 16) & 0xFF) == 254:
            return True
        
        return False
    except Exception:
        return False

def fetch_page_metadata(page_url: str) -> dict:
    """
    Fetches the title and description metadata from the specified web page URL.

    Args:
        page_url: A valid URL pointing to a public web page. Example: 'https://example.com/article'

    Returns:
        A dictionary containing the page's title and description, e.g., {'title': 'Example', 'description': 'An example page'}
    """
    # Validate the URL to prevent SSRF attacks
    if not _is_safe_url(page_url):
        return {
            'title': 'Content unavailable',
            'description': 'Invalid URL provided'
        }

    try:
        # Create SSL context with proper certificate verification
        context = ssl.create_default_context(cafile=certifi.where())
        
        # Make the request with a timeout to prevent hanging connections
        request = urllib.request.Request(
            page_url,
            headers={'User-Agent': 'Mozilla/5.0 (compatible; SocialMediaScraper/1.0)'}
        )
        with urllib.request.urlopen(request, context=context, timeout=10) as response:
            content = response.read().decode('utf-8')

        # Extract title
        title_match = re.search(r'<title>(.*?)</title>', content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL)
        title = title_match.group(1).strip() if title_match else "No title found"

        # Extract meta description
        desc_match = re.search(r'<meta[^>]*name=["\']description["\'][^>]*content=["\'](.*?)["\']',
                              content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL)
        description = desc_match.group(1).strip() if desc_match else "No description available"

        return {
            'title': title,
            'description': description
        }

    except (urllib.error.URLError, urllib.error.HTTPError, UnicodeDecodeError, ValueError):
        # Return default values if the page cannot be fetched or parsed
        return {
            'title': 'Content unavailable',
            'description': 'Unable to fetch page content'
        }


def _is_safe_url(url: str) -> bool:
    """
    Validates that the URL is safe to request (prevents SSRF).
    
    Args:
        url: URL string to validate
        
    Returns:
        True if the URL is safe, False otherwise
    """
    try:
        parsed = urlparse(url)
        
        # Check if scheme is http or https
        if parsed.scheme not in ('http', 'https'):
            return False
            
        # Get the hostname
        hostname = parsed.hostname
        if not hostname:
            return False
            
        # Resolve hostname to IP address to check if it's a private/internal address
        try:
            ip_addr = ipaddress.ip_address(socket.gethostbyname(hostname))
            # Block private IP addresses
            if ip_addr.is_private or ip_addr.is_loopback or ip_addr.is_link_local:
                return False
        except socket.gaierror:
            # If DNS resolution fails, we still want to validate the hostname
            pass
            
        # Block common internal hostnames
        if hostname in ('localhost', '127.0.0.1', '::1') or \
           hostname.startswith('127.') or \
           hostname.startswith('10.') or \
           hostname.startswith('192.168.') or \
           hostname.startswith('172.') and 16 <= int(hostname.split('.')[1]) <= 31:
            return False
            
        # Block AWS metadata service
        if hostname == '169.254.169.254' or hostname.endswith('.internal'):
            return False
            
        return True
    except Exception:
        return False

import urllib.request
import urllib.error
from urllib.parse import urlparse
from typing import Dict, Any
import re
import ssl
import certifi


def fetch_page_metadata(page_url: str) -> dict:
    """
    Fetches the title and description metadata from the specified web page URL.

    Args:
        page_url: A valid URL pointing to a public web page. Example: 'https://example.com/article'

    Returns:
        A dictionary containing the page's title and description, e.g., {'title': 'Example', 'description': 'An example page'}
    """
    # Validate the URL scheme to ensure it's an HTTP/HTTPS URL
    parsed_url = urlparse(page_url)
    if parsed_url.scheme not in ['http', 'https']:
        return {
            'title': 'Content unavailable',
            'description': 'Invalid URL scheme'
        }
    
    try:
        # Set a reasonable timeout and fetch the content of the specified URL
        context = ssl.create_default_context(cafile=certifi.where())
        with urllib.request.urlopen(page_url, timeout=10, context=context) as response:
            content = response.read().decode('utf-8')

        # Extract title - using more secure regex pattern that limits repetition
        # Match up to 1000 characters between <title> tags to prevent ReDoS
        title_match = re.search(r'<title>(.{1,1000}?)</title>', content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL)
        title = title_match.group(1).strip() if title_match else "No title found"

        # Extract meta description - using more secure regex pattern that limits repetition
        # Limit the number of characters between tags to prevent ReDoS
        desc_match = re.search(
            r'<meta[^>]{1,500}?name=["\']description["\'][^>]{1,500}?content=["\'](.{1,1000}?)["\']',
            content, re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL)
        description = desc_match.group(1).strip() if desc_match else "No description available"

        # Sanitize the extracted text by removing potential harmful content
        title = _sanitize_text(title)
        description = _sanitize_text(description)

        return {
            'title': title,
            'description': description
        }

    except (urllib.error.URLError, urllib.error.HTTPError, UnicodeDecodeError, ValueError, OSError):
        # If the page cannot be fetched or parsed, return default values
        return {
            'title': 'Content unavailable',
            'description': 'Unable to fetch page content'
        }


def _sanitize_text(text: str) -> str:
    """Helper function to sanitize extracted text."""
    # Remove any potential script tags or other harmful content
    # This is a basic sanitization - for production use consider using a proper HTML sanitizer
    sanitized = re.sub(r'<script[^>]*>.*?</script>', '', text, flags=re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL)
    sanitized = re.sub(r'<!--.*?-->', '', sanitized, flags=re.DOTALL)  # Remove comments
    return sanitized.strip()

import urllib.request
import urllib.error
from urllib.parse import urlparse
from typing import Dict, Any
import re
import ssl
import certifi


def fetch_page_metadata(page_url: str) -> dict:
    """
    Fetches the title and description metadata from the specified web page URL.

    Args:
        page_url: A valid URL pointing to a public web page. Example: 'https://example.com/article'

    Returns:
        A dictionary containing the page's title and description, e.g., {'title': 'Example', 'description': 'An example page'}
    """
    # Validate URL format and scheme
    parsed_url = urlparse(page_url)
    if not all([parsed_url.scheme in ('http', 'https'), parsed_url.netloc]):
        return {
            'title': 'Content unavailable',
            'description': 'Invalid URL format'
        }
    
    # Prevent SSRF by checking for private/internal IP ranges
    hostname = parsed_url.hostname
    if hostname:
        # Check for private IP addresses or local addresses
        if hostname.startswith(('10.', '172.', '192.168.')) or \
           hostname in ('localhost', '127.0.0.1', '0.0.0.0') or \
           hostname.endswith(('.internal', '.local')):
            return {
                'title': 'Content unavailable',
                'description': 'URL not accessible'
            }
    
    try:
        # Create SSL context with certificate verification
        context = ssl.create_default_context(cafile=certifi.where())
        
        # Make request with timeout to prevent hanging
        request = urllib.request.Request(
            page_url,
            headers={'User-Agent': 'Mozilla/5.0 (compatible; SocialMediaScraper/1.0)'}
        )
        
        with urllib.request.urlopen(request, context=context, timeout=10) as response:
            content = response.read().decode('utf-8', errors='ignore')

        # Extract title - more robust regex pattern
        title_match = re.search(r'<title[^>]*>([^<]*)</title>', content, re.IGNORECASE)
        title = title_match.group(1).strip() if title_match else "No title found"
        
        # Limit title length to prevent extremely long titles
        title = title[:200]

        # Extract meta description - look for both name and property attributes
        desc_match = re.search(
            r'<meta[^>]*(?:name|property)=["\'](?:description|og:description)["\'][^>]*content=["\']([^"\']*)["\']',
            content, re.IGNORECASE
        )
        description = desc_match.group(1).strip() if desc_match else "No description available"
        
        # Limit description length to prevent extremely long descriptions
        description = description[:500]

        return {
            'title': title,
            'description': description
        }

    except (urllib.error.URLError, urllib.error.HTTPError, UnicodeDecodeError, ValueError):
        # If the page cannot be fetched or parsed, return default values
        return {
            'title': 'Content unavailable',
            'description': 'Unable to fetch page content'
        }

        if not parsed_url.netloc:

        # Resolve hostname to IP to check if it's a private/internal address

        try:

            if ip_addr.is_private or ip_addr.is_loopback or ip_addr.is_link_local:

        except ValueError:

            ip_addr = ipaddress.ip_address(resolved_ip)
            if ip_addr.is_private or ip_addr.is_loopback or ip_addr.is_link_local:
                raise ValueError("Access to private addresses is not allowed")

        # Block common internal/local patterns in hostname
        blocked_hostnames = [
            'localhost',
            'local',
            'internal',
        ]
        
        lower_hostname = hostname.lower()
        for blocked in blocked_hostnames:
            if blocked in lower_hostname:
                raise ValueError(f"Access to {blocked} addresses is not allowed")
                
    except (ValueError, socket.gaierror):

    """Custom HTML parser to extract title and meta description."""
    
    def __init__(self):
        super().__init__()
        self.title = ""
        self.description = ""
        self.in_title_tag = False
        self.current_tag = ""
        self.current_attrs = {}

    def handle_starttag(self, tag: str, attrs):
        self.current_tag = tag
        self.current_attrs = dict(attrs)
        
        # Look for meta tags with name="description" or property="og:description"
        if tag == 'meta':
            attrs_dict = dict(attrs)
            if (attrs_dict.get('name', '').lower() == 'description' or

                content = attrs_dict.get('content', '')
                if content and not self.description:

        elif tag == 'title':
            self.in_title_tag = True

    def handle_endtag(self, tag: str):
        if tag == 'title':

        # Prevent file:// and other potentially dangerous schemes
        if not parsed.netloc:

        # Basic check to prevent obvious local network access attempts

        if not host:

        # Block private IP ranges to prevent internal network access
        if host.startswith(('10.', '172.', '192.')) or host == 'localhost':

            pass  # We'll still process the request but with caution
        return True
    except Exception:
        return False


def fetch_page_metadata(page_url: str) -> Dict[str, str]:
    """
    Fetches metadata (title and description) from a given webpage URL.

        A dictionary containing 'title' and 'description' keys
